tweets.title: Telangana HC Orders Army to Guard Disputed Land In an extraordinary order, the Telangana High Court directed the Indian Army to deploy personnel to protect a 40-acre disputed property after alleging repeated violations of court orders by HYDRAA. Justice Anil Kumar said the court had "lost faith in the state and its instrumentalities" and asked the Registrar General to coordinate with the Army for the deployment of 10 personnel. The court also warned that HYDRAA Commissioner A. Ranganath could face custody for alleged repeated contempt of court. The State government has denied the allegations, and the land ownership dispute remains pending before the court.
tweets.content:

In an extraordinary order, the Telangana High Court directed the Indian Army to deploy personnel to protect a 40-acre disputed property after alleging repeated violations of court orders by HYDRAA.

tweets.title: In the Bab-al-Mandab / Red Sea area, the situation has deteriorated following the declaration against Saudi-linked shipping, says India's info fusion center that keeps an eye on the Indian ocean.
tweets.content:
In the Bab-al-Mandab / Red Sea area, the situation has deteriorated following the declaration against Saudi-linked shipping, says India's info fusion center that keeps an eye on the Indian ocean.

IFC-IOR (@IFC_IOR)

🚨 #IFCIOR | REGIONAL MARITIME SECURITY ADVISORY UPDATE 12/26 | AS ON 240700Z JUL 26

The maritime security environment across the #StraitofHormuz, #GulfofAden, #BabalMandab and #RedSea remains HIGHLY VOLATILE, with concurrent hybrid threats, piracy activity and route-specific risks affecting commercial shipping.

📌 Since the advisory issued on 02 Jul 26, IFC-IOR has monitored 12 attacks on merchant ships in the Strait of Hormuz / Persian Gulf / Gulf of Oman area. One seafarer has been reported killed, one missing and eighteen injured.

⚠️ The Strait of Hormuz threat environment remains CRITICAL. GNSS / GPS and AIS interference and attacks on commercial shipping continue to affect navigational safety. Non-essential transit through the Strait of Hormuz should be reconsidered until the situation stabilises.

🌊 In the Bab-al-Mandab / Red Sea area, the situation has deteriorated following the declaration against Saudi-linked shipping. Since the declaration, 13 vessels have turned back without reaching their intended destinations, and on 23 Jul 26, one Saudi Arabia-linked tanker was reportedly attacked in the Red Sea.

🏴‍☠️ In the Gulf of Aden/ Yemen approaches, piracy and armed robbery activity remains active. Since 02 Jul 26, IFC-IOR has monitored 03 piracy-related incidents, including the hijacking of tanker ASANA and two suspicious approaches that were thwarted.

🛡️ CSOs, Masters and operators are advised to conduct voyage-specific threat assessments, verify war-risk insurance and Flag State requirements, maintain AIS/radar/navigation lights as appropriate, monitor VHF Ch 16 and promulgated V/UHF channels, maintain independent positioning capability, harden vessels, rehearse citadel and emergency procedures, and comply with BMP (MS) and recognised reporting procedures.

📢 Vessels operating in or intending to transit affected areas are advised to report to IFC-IOR and relevant regional maritime security centres and to report suspicious activity, attacks, approaches, GNSS/ AIS anomalies or security concerns without delay.

tweets.title: The Indian Army has formally begun raising its first five Integrated Battle Groups (IBGs) and a Fire Support Group under the 17 Mountain Strike Corps in the Eastern Command, enhancing rapid-response capabilities along the China front. Each IBG is a self-contained, combined-arms formation of around 5,000 personnel, integrating infantry, armour, artillery, air defence, engineers and logistics. Designed for deployment within 12–48 hours, the formations are tailored to the Threat, Terrain and Task of each operational sector. Originally conceived with Pakistan in mind, the first IBGs have instead been deployed opposite China in Arunachal Pradesh and Sikkim, reflecting India's evolving security priorities following the 2020 LAC standoff. Military analysts view the IBGs as the institutional realization, and modernization, of India's long-discussed "Cold Start" concept, providing New Delhi with a credible option for rapid, limited conventional responses below the threshold of prolonged mobilization.
tweets.content:
The Indian Army has formally begun raising its first five Integrated Battle Groups (IBGs) and a Fire Support Group under the 17 Mountain Strike Corps in the Eastern Command, enhancing rapid-response capabilities along the China front.

Each IBG is a self-contained, combined-arms formation of around 5,000 personnel, integrating infantry, armour, artillery, air defence, engineers and logistics. Designed for deployment within 12–48 hours, the formations are tailored to the Threat, Terrain and Task of each operational sector.

Originally conceived with Pakistan in mind, the first IBGs have instead been deployed opposite China in Arunachal Pradesh and Sikkim, reflecting India's evolving security priorities following the 2020 LAC standoff.

Military analysts view the IBGs as the institutional realization, and modernization, of India's long-discussed "Cold Start" concept, providing New Delhi with a credible option for rapid, limited conventional responses below the threshold of prolonged mobilization.

India's DRDO has successfully carried out the first flight test of the Kusha Long-Range Surface-to-Air Missile (LRSAM) from APJ Abdul Kalam Island, Odisha.

The missile intercepted a high-speed, high-altitude aerial target, validating its ability to engage fighter aircraft, missiles, UAVs, and large enemy aircraft across a wide range and altitude envelope.

Only a handful of nations possess the capability to develop such long-range air defence systems. The Kusha programme is expected to reduce import dependence and significantly strengthen India's indigenous air defence network.

tweets.title: India Targets 5 Indigenous Small Modular Reactors by 2033 The Government has announced plans to make at least five indigenous Small Modular Reactors (SMRs) operational by 2033, as part of its long-term nuclear energy strategy. BARC is developing a 220 MW Bharat SMR, a 55 MW SMR, and a high-temperature gas-cooled reactor capable of producing hydrogen. India aims to expand its nuclear power capacity from 8.78 GW today to ~22 GW by 2031–32, with a long-term target of 100 GW by 2047 under the Nuclear Energy Mission.

tweets.title: India's Bharat Forge has signed an MoU with FLYING WHALES of France-Canada to jointly develop and manufacture heavy-lift airships in India for defence and strategic applications. The partnership will focus on the LCA60T platform—a next-generation airship capable of carrying up to 60 tonnes, featuring vertical take-off and landing (VTOL), hybrid-electric propulsion, and operations with minimal ground infrastructure. The airships are expected to support: 🔹 Logistics to forward military bases 🔹 Transport of heavy equipment and supplies 🔹 ISR (Intelligence, Surveillance & Reconnaissance) missions 🔹 Communication relay 🔹 Humanitarian assistance & disaster relief 🔹 Rapid deployment in remote border regions
tweets.content:
India's Bharat Forge has signed an MoU with FLYING WHALES of France-Canada to jointly develop and manufacture heavy-lift airships in India for defence and strategic applications.

The partnership will focus on the LCA60T platform—a next-generation airship capable of carrying up to 60 tonnes, featuring vertical take-off and landing (VTOL), hybrid-electric propulsion, and operations with minimal ground infrastructure.

The airships are expected to support:
🔹 Logistics to forward military bases
🔹 Transport of heavy equipment and supplies
🔹 ISR (Intelligence, Surveillance & Reconnaissance) missions
🔹 Communication relay
🔹 Humanitarian assistance & disaster relief
🔹 Rapid deployment in remote border regions

Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf has warned that if Iran is prevented from exporting oil, no country in the region will be able to export oil either.

The warning follows US President Donald Trump's threat to strike Iranian bridges and power plants if Iran attacks commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

The escalating rhetoric comes amid continued US-Iran military tensions, raising fresh concerns over global energy supplies and maritime security in the Gulf.

Delhi Police is outnumbered when it comes to tackling a protest of 1 lakh+ people, which is now growing every day. The total strength of Delhi Police is around 83,000 personnel, responsible for policing 1,484 square kilometres. In peacetime, one Delhi Police personnel is outnumbered by approximately 259 civilians, let alone during a protest of this scale. A mob tends to develop a mentality to flank and attack when it sees security forces being outnumbered. To assert control when outnumbered, they have to use force.
